title: Nania rubiginosa
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Nania rubiginosa." 

However, I found that you may be referring to "Nardostachys grandiflora" or "Nardostachys jatamansi" which is commonly known as Nania or Spikenard. 

Here's a paragraph about Nardostachys jatamansi:

Nardostachys jatamansi, commonly known as Spikenard, is a plant species in the family Valerianaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and sedative properties, which can help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Nardostachys jatamansi include sesquiterpenoid lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Nardostachys jatamansi is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues.
